before.title: Without CodyMaster
items: Can't find old specs in 200+ files

bornFromPain.label: WHY WE BUILT THIS
bornFromPain.title:
Born from real pain,
not a business plan
bornFromPain.subtitle: 10+ years of product experience + 6 months of non-stop vibe coding. Every skill exists because of a real failure that cost real time and real tears.
bornFromPain.quote: "I'm a Head of Product with 10+ years of experience. I can't code. AI gave me 100 hands — but without discipline, those hands created chaos. So I built the discipline."
bornFromPain.authorRole: Head of Product · 10+ Years · Creator of CodyMaster

cards.title: Design Chaos
cards.pain: AI designed every project differently. Same brand, 3 different styles. Clients thought it was 3 different companies.

cards.title: The Week AI Deleted Everything
cards.pain: During an i18n refactor, AI lost context and deleted a week's worth of code. Had to redo it 4 times. Fix one thing, break another.

cards.title: Great Product, Zero Users
cards.pain: Built amazing apps but nobody knew how to use them. No docs, no onboarding, no self-service. Support team overwhelmed.

cards.title: AI That Never Learns
cards.pain: Same mistake every session. Explain the same codebase every morning. AI has zero memory, zero growth. It's like training a new intern every single day.

hero.title: Smart brain, self-learning. Not a goldfish brain.
hero.subtitle: CodyMaster is the only AI with smart memory inspired by neuroscience. Remembers past mistakes, forgets what's irrelevant, self-repairs when memory causes bugs. No repeating errors. No losing context. Doesn't get dumber over time.

problem.label: THE PROBLEM
problem.title: AI Without Memory = Disaster
problem.subtitle: Most AI tools have a "goldfish brain" — every session starts from zero.

cards.title: Repeating the Same Mistakes
cards.desc: Fixed CORS once? It'll break it the exact same way next time. Zero learning.

cards.title: Contradicting Itself
cards.desc: Session 1: "Use REST." Session 2: "Use GraphQL." No consistency, conflicting decisions.

cards.title: Burning Tokens
cards.desc: Re-explaining the same project context every session = wasting tokens and money.

tiers.label: FIVE-TIER MEMORY
tiers.title:
Our brain knows what to remember,
what to forget, and what to keep forever.
tiers.subtitle: Inspired by neuroscience: different memories have different lifespans.

cards.title: Sensory Memory
cards.desc: Temporary info within 1 command: "file X has 200 lines." Forgotten immediately.
cards.analogy: 🧠 Like hearing a phone number once — gone instantly

cards.title: Working Memory
cards.desc: "Currently fixing auth, goal is deploy." Remembers what you're thinking and doing.
cards.analogy: 🧠 Like your grocery list today — needed now

cards.title: Long-Term Memory
cards.desc: "CORS error due to missing proxy." Lasting lessons — kept only if reinforced ≥ 2 times.
cards.analogy: 🧠 Like riding a bike — never forgotten

cards.title: Semantic Memory
cards.desc: Deep search across massive docs without token limits. Finds concepts and patterns hidden in text.

cards.title: Structural Memory
cards.desc: Understands your codebase architecture. 95% token compression by reading AST, not raw code.
cards.analogy: 🧠 Like an innate sense of spatial awareness

decay.label: EBBINGHAUS FORGETTING CURVE
decay.title: Forget smart, not forget everything.
decay.subtitle: Learnings not reinforced naturally expire. Reinforced ones last longer. Like the human brain.

heal.label: SELF-HEALING MEMORY
heal.title:
If memory causes bugs,
it fixes itself.
heal.subtitle: Before debugging, CodyMaster checks whether its own memory is the source of the problem.

steps.title: Bug Detected
steps.desc: Test fails, deploy errors, or unexpected behavior detected.

steps.title: Suspect Memory
steps.desc: Before blaming code, check: is outdated learning causing this?

steps.title: Investigate
steps.desc: Cross-reference learnings with actual codebase. Find conflicts.

steps.title: Heal & Update
steps.desc: Delete or update wrong memory. Log the correction. Prevent loops.

scope.label: SCOPE FILTERING
scope.title:
Load only what matters.
Save tokens, stay accurate.
scope.subtitle: Each learning is tagged (global, module:auth, file:routes.ts). Only relevant memories are loaded per task.

cta.title: Give your AI a real brain.
cta.subtitle: AI without memory = goldfish that talks. CodyMaster Brain turns AI into a colleague who remembers, learns fast, and never repeats the same mistakes.

cli_auto_16: For developers who think in terminal commands. Every slash command, every workflow, all in one place.

meta.title: Complete Guide — CodyMaster Kit | Personas, JTBD, Use Cases
meta.description: The complete guide to CodyMaster Kit: 5 user personas, 5 cm-jtbd, 8 real-world use cases. Learn who it's for, what problems it solves, and how it works.

docsPage.heroSub: Who is CodyMaster for? What problems does it solve? How does it actually work? Everything you need to know — in one page.

personasSection.label: WHO IS IT FOR?
personasSection.title: 5 User Personas
personasSection.subtitle: CodyMaster was built for real people with real problems. Find yourself below.

personas.tagline: Has ideas. No dev team. No coding skills.
personas.demographics: Solo founders, bootstrappers, first-time entrepreneurs. Age 25-45. No technical background.
painPoints: Can't turn ideas into working products without a developer
Agencies charge $10K-50K and take months
AI tools produce throwaway prototypes, not production code
No way to maintain quality or consistency across projects
personas.dreamOutcome: Build and launch a professional product — landing page, SaaS app, or content site — in days, not months, without writing a single line of code.
personas.jtbd: When I have a product idea but no technical skills, I want to describe it in plain language and get a production-ready product, so I can test the market before competitors move.

personas.tagline: Manages AI agents. Drowning in chaos.
personas.demographics: Product Managers, Business Owners with 5-15 years experience. Use AI daily but struggle with consistency.
painPoints: AI designs every project differently — same brand, 3 different styles
AI loses context and breaks existing code during refactors
No documentation for products, users can't self-onboard
Can't track what multiple AI agents are doing simultaneously
personas.dreamOutcome: A disciplined AI system that follows rules, maintains brand consistency, protects code, generates docs, and shows progress on a single dashboard.
personas.jtbd: When AI writes code inconsistently and breaks things, I want enforced quality standards and design system compliance, so nothing reaches production without passing quality gates.

personas.tagline: Tired of boilerplate. Wants to ship faster.
personas.demographics: Full-stack developers, freelancers. 3-10 years experience. Work solo or in small teams.
painPoints: 80% of time spent on boilerplate setup, not actual features
Every new project: setup git, deploy, i18n, test infra — from scratch
AI-generated code has no tests, no security scans
Deploying is stressful — no rollback, no quality gates
personas.dreamOutcome: Focus on architecture and business logic while AI handles boilerplate, tests, deploys, and documentation — with quality gates at every step.
personas.jtbd: When I start a new project, I want automated scaffolding with test infra, CI/CD, and design system from day one, so I can focus on business logic instead of setup.

personas.tagline: Needs content and landing pages. Yesterday.
personas.demographics: Marketing managers, growth hackers, content strategists. Manage brands and campaigns across channels.
painPoints: Content team can't produce articles fast enough
Landing pages take weeks through designers and developers
SEO optimization requires expensive specialists
No conversion tracking setup — can't measure ROAS
personas.dreamOutcome: Generate SEO-optimized content, high-converting landing pages, and complete tracking setup — all in minutes, using proven frameworks like StoryBrand and Cialdini.
personas.jtbd: When I need content marketing assets quickly, I want AI that writes using proven persuasion frameworks with SEO built in, so I can drive traffic without hiring a full content team.

personas.tagline: Building for multiple clients. Needs efficiency.
personas.demographics: Digital agencies, freelance developers, consultants. Juggling 3-10 client projects simultaneously.
painPoints: Every client project needs unique setup — repeating the same work
Design inconsistency across client projects damages credibility
Secret management nightmare — risk deploying to wrong client's account
Documentation and handoff takes longer than building
personas.dreamOutcome: One kit that bootstraps every client project with the same quality standard, enforces brand consistency, prevents cross-project contamination, and auto-generates handoff docs.
personas.jtbd: When I manage multiple client projects, I want a standardized toolkit that prevents cross-project mistakes and auto-generates documentation, so I can deliver faster with higher quality.

jtbdSection.label: WHAT PROBLEMS DOES IT SOLVE?
jtbdSection.title: 5 Jobs to Be Done
jtbdSection.subtitle: Every skill in CodyMaster exists because of a real job that real people need done. Here are the 5 core jobs.

jobs.situation: When I have a product idea but no technical skills...
jobs.motivation: I want to describe what I need in plain language and get a production-ready result...
jobs.outcome: So I can test the market before competitors move, without spending months or $50K on agencies.
jobs.functional: Build a working product from a text description
jobs.emotional: Feel empowered and capable despite not knowing code
jobs.social: Be seen as a competent founder who ships fast
jobs.push: Ideas stay in my head for months. Agencies are too expensive. No-code tools are too limited.
jobs.pull: Describe → Design → Code → Deploy in one session. Professional quality. Full ownership.
jobs.habit: Hiring freelancers, using Wix/Squarespace, or giving up on the idea entirely.
jobs.anxiety: Will the AI-generated code actually be production-ready? Can I maintain it long-term?

jobs.situation: When AI writes code inconsistently and breaks existing work...
jobs.motivation: I want enforced quality standards, design system compliance, and multi-layer protections...
jobs.outcome: So nothing breaks in production and every project maintains brand consistency.
jobs.functional: Enforce code quality, design rules, and testing automatically
jobs.emotional: Sleep well knowing the deployment pipeline catches errors
jobs.social: Deliver professional, consistent work every time
jobs.push: AI designs every project differently. Refactors delete existing code. No tests means no safety net.
jobs.pull: 6-gate quality system. Design system enforcement. Auto-rollback on failure.
jobs.habit: Manually reviewing AI output, redoing work 3-4 times, avoiding refactors.
jobs.anxiety: Will adding quality gates slow down my workflow? Is the overhead worth it?

jobs.situation: When I build great products that nobody knows how to use...
jobs.motivation: I want auto-generated documentation — API reference, SOPs, user guides, personas...
jobs.outcome: So users can self-onboard, support team isn't overwhelmed, and the product thrives.
jobs.functional: Generate documentation from codebase analysis automatically
jobs.emotional: Remove the dread of writing docs after exhausting coding sessions
jobs.social: Be recognized for having world-class documentation
jobs.push: No docs = no self-service = support team drowning. Writing docs after coding is torture.
jobs.pull: One scan of the codebase = complete knowledge base. Multi-language. SEO-optimized.
jobs.habit: Skipping docs entirely, or writing minimal README files.
jobs.anxiety: Will auto-generated docs be accurate enough? Will they match the codebase?

jobs.situation: When I run multiple AI agents across multiple projects...
jobs.motivation: I want a single control center to monitor progress, token usage, and deployment status...
jobs.outcome: So I don't lose track and can maximize productivity across all my work.
jobs.functional: Visual dashboard showing all agents, tasks, and deployments
jobs.emotional: Feel in control even when running 5+ projects simultaneously
jobs.social: Demonstrate operational maturity to clients and stakeholders
jobs.push: Can't see what's happening. Which agent is stuck? How many tokens spent? What deployed where?
jobs.pull: One screen shows everything: pipeline progress, task queue, token tracker, event log.
jobs.habit: Checking logs manually, running CLI commands, losing hours to context switching.
jobs.anxiety: Will the dashboard add complexity? Do I need to learn a new tool?

jobs.situation: When I need content marketing assets but can't hire a full team...
jobs.motivation: I want AI that writes using proven persuasion frameworks with SEO built in...
jobs.outcome: So I can drive traffic, generate leads, and scale content without hiring writers.
jobs.functional: Generate SEO blog posts, landing pages, ad copy, email campaigns
jobs.emotional: Confidence that content follows proven frameworks (not random AI output)
jobs.social: Build authority in the niche through consistent, high-quality publishing
jobs.push: Content team can't keep up. SEO specialists cost $5K/month. Tracking is a mess.
jobs.pull: StoryBrand + Cialdini + 7 frameworks combined. Auto-SEO. Complete tracking setup.
jobs.habit: Writing sporadically, no framework, no tracking, hoping for organic growth.
jobs.anxiety: Will AI-written content feel generic? Will Google penalize it?

useCasesSection.label: HOW DOES IT WORK IN PRACTICE?
useCasesSection.title: 8 Real-World Use Cases
useCasesSection.subtitle: Not theory. Here are real scenarios showing which skills activate, in what order, and what you get.

cases.title: Launch a Landing Page in 15 Minutes
cases.scenario: You have a coffee shop and need a professional website with online ordering. You describe it to CodyMaster in plain English.
cases.outcome: A fully responsive, SEO-optimized landing page with CRO elements, deployed to a live URL.

cases.title: Build a SaaS MVP in One Day
cases.scenario: You're validating a B2B idea — need auth, dashboard, API, and database. You describe the features and let CodyMaster plan and execute.
cases.outcome: A full-stack application with user authentication, admin dashboard, REST API, and PostgreSQL — tested and deployed.

cases.title: Auto-Generate Complete Documentation
cases.scenario: Your SaaS product has 50+ features but zero documentation. Users can't self-onboard. Support tickets are piling up.
cases.outcome: Full documentation site: API reference, user guides, SOPs, personas, JTBD analysis, process flows. Multi-language. SEO-optimized.

cases.title: Run a Content Marketing Campaign
cases.scenario: You need 30 blog posts, 5 landing pages, and conversion tracking across Facebook, Google, and TikTok — all optimized for SEO.
cases.outcome: 30 SEO-optimized articles, 5 high-converting landing pages, complete pixel setup with UTM tracking and attribution.

cases.title: Migrate to Multi-Language Safely
cases.scenario: Your app is English-only. You need Vietnamese, Chinese, and Russian — but the last i18n attempt broke everything.
cases.outcome: Complete multi-language support with 8 audit gates, HTML integrity checks, and zero regression. All tests pass.

cases.title: Security Audit and Safe Deploy
cases.scenario: Before launching v2.0, you need to ensure no secrets are leaked, all dependencies are secure, and deploy has rollback.
cases.outcome: Complete security audit: secret scanning, dependency check, identity verification, 8-gate deploy pipeline with automatic rollback.

cases.title: Bootstrap a New Client Project
cases.scenario: You're a freelancer starting a new client project. You need design system, test infrastructure, CI/CD, i18n, SEO — the works.
cases.outcome: Complete project scaffold: design system, test infra, deploy pipeline, i18n support, SEO foundation, AGENTS.md manifest — all in one command.

cases.title: Enforce Design System Across Projects
cases.scenario: Your brand has a design system but AI ignores it. Every AI-generated page looks different from your brand guidelines.
cases.outcome: AI reads your design tokens, components, and brand guidelines. Preview designs before coding. Every page matches your brand perfectly.

skillMapSection.label: HOW SKILLS CONNECT
skillMapSection.title: The Skill Map
skillMapSection.subtitle: 34 skills organized into 5 swarms. Skills auto-chain based on your task.

questions.q: Do I need to know how to code?
questions.a: No. CodyMaster is designed for people who can't code. You describe what you want in plain language — AI handles the rest.
Yes, CodyMaster is open source and free. You only pay for the AI provider (Gemini, Claude, etc.) you choose to use.

questions.q: Which AI agents does it work with?
questions.a: All major AI coding agents: Google Antigravity (Gemini), Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline/RooCode, GitHub Copilot, OpenClaw, and more.

questions.q: Can I use my existing design system?
questions.a: Yes! cm-ux-master reads your existing design tokens, components, and brand guidelines — then builds with them.

questions.q: How is this different from v0, Bolt, or Lovable?
questions.a: Those tools generate one-off prototypes. CodyMaster is a complete skills framework — it enforces TDD, quality gates, security scans, design consistency, documentation, and safe deployment. It's production-ready from day one.

questions.q: Can I create my own skills?
questions.a: Absolutely! Use cm-skill-mastery to learn how to create custom skills. Just follow the SKILL.md format — it's compatible with Antigravity, Claude, Cursor, and OpenClaw.

ctaSection.title:
Ready to solve your biggest
product challenge?
ctaSection.subtitle: Pick your persona. Choose your use case. Start building.

playbook_auto_107: When Things Don't Go as Planned
playbook_auto_108: Hit a wall? Here's the fix for every common problem.
playbook_auto_110: AI forgot what it was doing
playbook_auto_111: Long chats fill up the AI's memory. It "forgets" earlier instructions and starts going off track.
playbook_auto_113: Saves context to a file. AI reads it at the start of every session — never loses track again.
playbook_auto_115: Plan turns out to be wrong
playbook_auto_116: You're halfway done and realize the approach won't work, or requirements changed.
playbook_auto_118: Go back and update the plan. It's cheaper to rewrite a plan than to rewrite code.
playbook_auto_121: The AI guesses randomly instead of understanding why it's broken. Each attempt makes it worse.
playbook_auto_123: Forces AI to investigate the root cause first — no code changes until the "why" is clear.
playbook_auto_126: Multiple GitHub/Cloudflare accounts? One wrong push = your code ends up in someone else's project.
playbook_auto_128: Checks which account is active before every push. Blocks the action if it's the wrong one.
playbook_auto_130: AI only sees part of the project
playbook_auto_131: Large codebase (200+ files). AI reads a few files but misses the rest — changes break things it never saw.
playbook_auto_133: Adds semantic search so AI can find relevant code across the entire project instantly.
playbook_auto_136: AI puts passwords or API keys directly in code. Push to a public repo = instant security breach.
playbook_auto_138: Scans every commit automatically. Blocks the push if any secret is detected.
playbook_auto_141: Follow these and your AI-assisted development will be predictable, safe, and fast.
playbook_auto_17: "I want to do X — which skills do I use?" This page answers that question for every scenario you'll face.
playbook_auto_20: Find your goal below. Each card shows exactly which skills to use.
playbook_auto_22: "I want to build a new project"
playbook_auto_23: Start from zero: create repo, set up CI/CD, design system, and deploy — all in one session.
playbook_auto_31: Find the real cause first, write a test to prove it, fix it, then deploy safely.
playbook_auto_37: Analyze what to change, design with proven UX rules, preview the result, then build it.
playbook_auto_44: Extract text, translate in batches, and auto-audit to catch errors before they go live.
playbook_auto_48: Generate SEO articles, set up ad pixels (Meta, TikTok, Google), and measure what works.
playbook_auto_54: Scan your codebase and auto-generate docs, SOPs, and API references.
playbook_auto_58: Click any scenario to see the exact steps. Follow them in order.
playbook_auto_60: Build a New Project from Scratch
playbook_auto_61: Start from nothing. In 1-2 chat sessions you'll have a live project with tests, CI, and auto-deploy.
playbook_auto_74: Don't let AI guess randomly. This workflow finds the real cause first, proving it with a test before fixing anything.
playbook_auto_83: One wrong translation key = broken page. This skill extracts, translates, and double-checks everything automatically.
playbook_auto_85: Extract hardcoded strings → translate → audit → deploy
playbook_auto_88: Need blog articles + ad tracking? This chain auto-writes, quality-checks, deploys, and sets up tracking pixels.
playbook_auto_92: FB Pixel, TikTok, Google Ads setup
playbook_auto_97: Don't jump straight to code. Analyze what to change, design it properly, preview it visually, then plan the build.

meta.title: Get Started — CodyMaster
meta.description: Install CodyMaster on Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cursor, Codex and more. Get 34 AI skills in under 2 minutes.

startPage.heroSub: Choose your AI coding platform and get 34 skills in under 2 minutes.

steps.title: Install via NPM
steps.description: One command. Works on Mac, Linux, and Windows. Requires Node.js 18+.
steps.output:
✓ codymaster installed globally
📦 Commands available: cm, cody, codymaster

steps.title: Run Interactive Setup
steps.description: Auto-detects your AI tools (Claude, Gemini, Cursor…) and installs skills to each.
steps.output:
██████   CodyMaster v4.1.3
  █ CM █   34 Skills. Ship 10x faster.
  ██████   Choose your platform...

steps.title: Use CLI & Skills
steps.description: The CLI gives you dashboard, task management, and all 34 skills instantly.
steps.code:
cm dashboard
cm task add "My first task"
cm status

steps.title: Clone CodyMaster
steps.description: Clone the repository once to your home directory. This gives you all 34 skills.

steps.title: Copy Skills to Antigravity
steps.description: Copy all skills into your Antigravity skills directory.

steps.title: Add Skills to GEMINI.md
steps.description: Add skill references to your project's GEMINI.md so Antigravity loads them automatically.
steps.code:
# Add these lines to your project's GEMINI.md:
@~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/cm-skill-index/SKILL.md
@~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/cm-continuity/SKILL.md
@~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/cm-start/SKILL.md
steps.note: Skills activate automatically. Mention @cm-status anytime to see your current progress.

steps.title: Update Later
steps.description: Keep CodyMaster up to date by re-running the installer or pulling the latest.

steps.title: Get Claude Code
steps.description: CodyMaster runs as a Claude Code plugin. You need the Claude Code CLI installed first.
items: Works on Mac, Linux, and Windows (WSL)
steps.note: Already have Claude Code 1.0+? Skip to Step 2.

steps.title: Add the Marketplace
steps.description: Register the CodyMaster plugin source so Claude Code can find it.
steps.output:
✓ Marketplace registered: tody-agent/codymaster
✓ codymaster plugin available

steps.title: Install All 34 Skills
steps.description: One command installs everything — TDD, debugging, deployment, UX, content, security and more.
steps.output:
✓ cm v3.4.0 installed
📦 34 skills: cm-tdd, cm-debugging, cm-planning, cm-safe-deploy...

steps.title: Run Onboarding Tour
steps.description: Open Claude Code and run the interactive tour to see all 34 skills in action.
steps.output: 🚀 Welcome to CodyMaster! Let's explore all 34 skills...

steps.title: Install Gemini CLI
steps.description: Make sure you have the Gemini CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Clone CodyMaster
steps.description: Clone the repository and copy skills to the Gemini skills directory.

steps.title: Or Use Auto-Installer
steps.description: The auto-installer clones and copies everything in one command.

steps.title: Use Skills
steps.description: Skills activate automatically. Invoke explicitly with @[/skill-name] in any session.
steps.code: @[/cm-planning] Design a new feature for my app

steps.title: Update Later
steps.description: Keep CodyMaster up to date with one command.

steps.title: Open Cursor Agent Chat
steps.description: Open Cursor and switch to Agent mode in the chat panel.

steps.title: Install the Plugin
steps.description: Run the install command in Cursor agent chat — or search 'cody-master' in the marketplace.

steps.title: Use Skills
steps.description: Skills activate automatically based on context. Invoke explicitly with @mention.
steps.code: @cm-planning Design a new auth system for my app

steps.title: Start a Codex Session
steps.description: Open your Codex environment in the project root.
items: Open your terminal in the project root

steps.title: Fetch Install Instructions
steps.description: Tell Codex to fetch and follow the install guide from GitHub.
steps.output: ✓ Codex reads, parses, and installs all 34 skills automatically

steps.title: Use Skills
steps.description: Skills are now in .codex/skills/ and activate automatically.
steps.code: @cm-tdd Implement user authentication with full test coverage

steps.title: Clone CodyMaster
steps.description: Clone the repository once to your home directory.

steps.title: Copy Skills to Your Platform
steps.description: Drop the SKILL.md files into your platform's rules or skills directory.
steps.code:
# Windsurf
cp -r ~/.cody-master/skills/* ~/.windsurf/rules/

# Cline / RooCode
cp -r ~/.cody-master/skills/* ~/.cline/skills/

# Project-local
cp -r ~/.cody-master/skills/* .windsurf/rules/

steps.title: Use Skills
steps.description: Open your editor and invoke any skill. Skills activate automatically when relevant.
steps.code: @cm-planning Help me design a new feature

oneliner.label: �� Aider — AI pair programmer in your terminal

steps.title: Install Aider (if not installed)
steps.description: Aider is an AI pair programmer that runs in your terminal.

steps.title: Install CodyMaster skills
steps.description: Downloads all 34+ skills to ~/.aider/skills/ for use in Aider sessions.

steps.title: Load a skill in Aider
steps.description: Pass a skill file as context when starting an Aider session.
steps.code:
aider --read ~/.aider/skills/cm-planning/SKILL.md
# Or use multiple skills
aider --read ~/.aider/skills/cm-tdd/SKILL.md \
      --read ~/.aider/skills/cm-debugging/SKILL.md

oneliner.label: 🔗 Continue.dev — Open-source AI code assistant for VS Code & JetBrains

steps.title: Install CodyMaster rules
steps.description: Copies all skill files as rules to ~/.continue/rules/ — auto-loaded by Continue.dev.

steps.title: Verify in Continue.dev
steps.description: Rules in ~/.continue/rules/ are automatically picked up by Continue. Open VS Code and chat to verify.
steps.code:
ls ~/.continue/rules/ | head -10
# You should see: cm-planning.md, cm-debugging.md, cm-tdd.md ...

oneliner.label: ☁️ Amazon Q — AI assistant with AWS expertise

steps.title: Install Amazon Q CLI (if not installed)
steps.description: Amazon Q Developer is AWS's AI coding assistant.
steps.code:
# macOS
brew install amazon-q
# Or download from: https://aws.amazon.com/q/developer/

steps.title: Install CodyMaster skills
steps.description: Downloads skills to ~/.aws/amazonq/skills/ for use with Amazon Q.

steps.title: Use skills in Q chat
steps.description: Reference skill files as context when starting Q chat sessions.
steps.code:
q chat --context ~/.aws/amazonq/skills/cm-planning/SKILL.md
# Or q dev mode
q dev

steps.title: Install Amp (if not installed)
steps.description: Amp is an agentic coding assistant by Sourcegraph.

steps.title: Install CodyMaster skills
steps.description: Downloads all skills to ~/.amp/skills/ for use in Amp sessions.

steps.title: Add skills to your AGENTS.md
steps.description: Reference skills from your project's AGENTS.md so Amp loads them automatically.

steps.title: Clone the Repository
steps.description: Clone CodyMaster once to your home directory.

steps.title: Copy to Your Platform
steps.description: Drop the universal SKILL.md files into any platform's skills directory.
steps.code:
# Gemini (global)
cp -r ~/.cody-master/skills/* ~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/

# Gemini (project)
cp -r ~/.cody-master/skills/* .gemini/skills/

# OpenCode
cp -r ~/.cody-master/skills/* .opencode/skills/

# Kiro
cp -r ~/.cody-master/skills/* .kiro/skills/

steps.title: Verify & Use
steps.description: Open your AI agent and reference any skill. They activate automatically based on context.
steps.code: @cm-tdd Implement login with full test coverage
steps.note: Works on GitHub Copilot, Kiro, OpenCode, Windsurf, and any platform supporting custom instructions.

steps.title: Install OpenCode
steps.description: Make sure you have OpenCode installed and authenticated.
items: Install: npm install -g opencode-ai (or brew install opencode)
Run: opencode in your project directory

steps.title: Add Skills via npx
steps.description: Use the CodyMaster CLI to install skills directly into your OpenCode project.
steps.output:
📦 opencode — Installing all 34 skills
  ✅ cm-tdd
  ✅ cm-debugging
  ✅ cm-planning
  ... (34 total)
✅ 34 skills installed → ./.opencode/skills/

steps.title: Or clone manually

meta.title: Our Story — CodyMaster Kit
meta.description: The real story behind CodyMaster: born from a Product Manager's pain, built through hundreds of sleepless nights.

storyPage.heroSub: Not a pitch. Not marketing. This is what actually happened.
storyPage.founderIntro: I'm not a developer. I'm a Product Manager with 10+ years of experience. Code — the thing I can't do — used to be the tallest wall between me and turning ideas into reality.

chapters.title: When AI Gave Me 100 Hands
chapters.content:
Then AI appeared. Not 2 extra hands. But 100 hands. Suddenly I — someone who couldn't write a single JavaScript function — could build a full-stack application. Landing page? Done. API? Got it. Database schema? AI handles it.

It felt like someone who'd been riding a bicycle their whole life suddenly got to ride a rocket.

But a rocket without a control system is just... a flying bomb.

chapters.title: Design Chaos: Different Every Time
chapters.content:
I started using AI for company projects. Project A: minimalist, sans-serif, 8px radius. Project B — same system, same brand: suddenly AI switched to serif fonts, round buttons, completely different palette. Project C: yet another style entirely.

Every time, a different design. Never consistent.

As a 10+ year Product Manager, this drove me insane. Not because designs were ugly — sometimes each one looked great individually. But put them side by side and it was pure chaos. Business needs consistency. Brand needs a system. Clients looked at 3 pages and thought they were 3 different companies.
chapters.quote: AI is brilliant at design. It's just terrible at remembering the rules.

chapters.title: The Week AI Deleted Everything
chapters.content:
This is the most painful lesson. I will never forget it.

There was a large SaaS project — running in production, needed a full refactor for multi-language, multi-country support. I handed it to AI. It started... fine. Converting this file, translating that string.

Then everything collapsed.

Context got too long, AI started "losing its mind." It fixed one page, broke another. Got Vietnamese working, English disappeared. And the worst part — during one refactor pass, AI deleted nearly all the existing code. An entire week of work. Gone.

I had to redo it. Attempt 1. Attempt 2. Attempt 3. Attempt 4.

Each time, fix one thing, lose another. I sat in front of the screen at 2 AM, staring at endless diff logs, asking myself why I trusted a production system to AI without protection.

I swore: This will never happen again.
chapters.quote: 4 attempts. An entire week lost. That's when I built the multi-layer protection system.

chapters.title: The Lazy Person's Philosophy
chapters.content:
I'll be honest: I'm lazy.

Not lazy about thinking — lazy about doing the same thing over and over. Every new project: set up git, configure deploy, create design system, wire i18n, write README... Every time the same. Every time exhausting.

And I realized: Any problem I've faced twice should become an automated process by the third time.

So I started packaging every painful lesson into a reusable skill. Improve it, refine it, make it reproducible. Next time anyone hits the same wall, they just call one command.
chapters.quote: A problem encountered twice should never be encountered a third time.

chapters.title: Great Products Nobody Knows About
chapters.content:
10+ years as a Product Manager at a SaaS company, one obsession haunted me: Self-onboarding.

Amazing product. Tons of features. But new users came in and... got lost. No proper docs. No user guides. No API reference. Support team drowning in repeat questions.

And when I built my own products without a writing team: documentation became torture. Finish coding, completely exhausted, zero energy left to write a README.

So cm-dockit was born — a documentation powerhouse. It reads code, understands code, and auto-generates: API docs, SOPs, user guides, persona analysis, JTBD mapping, process flows. Multi-language. SEO-optimized. Package your product into world-class documentation without writing a single word.
chapters.quote: The best product in the world is worthless if nobody knows how to use it.

chapters.title: Mission Control for 100 Hands
chapters.content:
When you run multiple AI agents, multiple projects, multiple tasks in parallel to maximize productivity... you quickly lose control. Which task is done? Which agent is running? Which deploy went to production? How many tokens have been spent?

I'm a visual person. I need to SEE everything that's happening.

Everything on one screen: pipeline progress, task queue, token tracker, event log. No digging through logs. No running CLI commands. Open it — look — know the situation instantly.
chapters.quote: If you can't see it, you can't control it.

chapters.title: Gratitude to the Community
chapters.content:
Not everything here came from my head.

I owe the community a tremendous debt.

Superpower, Loki Mode, UX Max Pro... Brilliant skills that amazing people shared with the world — I learned from them, curated them, refined them, and integrated them into the CodyMaster ecosystem. Every skill in this toolkit isn't just code — it's collective intelligence.

Thank you to everyone who shared. Truly.
chapters.quote: Standing on the shoulders of a generous community.

closing.title: So What Is CodyMaster?
closing.content:
It's not just a plugin. Not a collection of prompts.

It's 10+ years of product management experience + 6 months of battle-tested vibe coding, distilled into an integrated operating system for your AI agent.

A brain that learns from its mistakes. A 4-layer defense system that protects your codebase. A full-cycle engine from Idea → Design → Code → Test → Deploy → Docs → Learn. A design system extractor that can rescue even the oldest products. A documentation engine that generates complete knowledge bases from code.

The answer to one single question:
closing.question: "How can someone who is lazy — brilliantly, strategically lazy — create world-class products without repeating the same mistakes, the same setup, the same pain?"
closing.punchline:
34 skills. Each skill is a lesson learned the hard way. Each lesson cost sleepless nights.

Install CodyMaster, and you're not adding skills — you're hiring an entire senior team. A team that remembers, learns, improves, and never ships broken code to production.

You don't have to go through those nights. We already did.

wordplay.label: The Wordplay
wordplay.desc: "Cody" comes from "Code Đi" (Cô-đi) — Vietnamese for "Go code!" An invitation to just start building, stop overthinking.

heart.label: The Heart
heart.desc: Cody is also the name of the creator's child. This kit carries personal meaning — every skill was crafted with the same care and dedication as raising a child.

philosophy.label: The Philosophy
philosophy.desc: CodyMaster = Code like an expert. Not just write code, but master the craft — with discipline, testing, planning, and quality at every step.

useCases.label: WHAT YOU CAN BUILD
useCases.title:
From ideas to products,
in every category
useCases.subtitle: Real scenarios, real results. Here's what people build with CodyMaster every day.

cases.title: Landing Pages
cases.description: High-converting pages with CRO optimization, A/B testing hooks, and pixel-perfect design. Deployed in minutes.

cases.title: SaaS Applications
cases.description: Full-stack apps with auth, dashboards, API, database, and multi-tenant architecture. Production-ready from day one.

cases.title: Documentation Sites
cases.description: Multi-language docs with API references, SOPs, user guides, and process flows. Auto-generated from your codebase.

cases.title: Content Marketing
cases.description: SEO-optimized blog posts, social content, and email campaigns. Powered by StoryBrand + Cialdini frameworks.

playbookSub: "I want to do X — which skills do I use?" This page answers that question for every scenario you'll face.
cliSub: For developers who think in terminal commands. Every slash command, every workflow, all in one place.

hero.title:
Parallel AI Coding
Without the Chaos
hero.sub: Run multiple AI agents simultaneously. Zero merge conflicts. Zero hidden bugs. 6 TRIZ inventive principles make parallel coding both fast AND reliable.

problem.label: THE PROBLEM
problem.title: Parallel AI Coding Is Broken
problem.sub: 2025-2026 industry research reveals a harsh truth: parallel AI agents produce code faster but create more problems than they solve.
problem.card1: Increase in PR review time. More code, but reviews can't keep up — quality bottleneck shifts downstream.
problem.card2: More static analysis warnings per session. Technical debt grows silently with every parallel run.
problem.card3: Of AI-generated code contains OWASP vulnerabilities. Speed without security scanning is reckless.
problem.card4: Of developers say AI code "looks correct but isn't." Hidden bugs bypass initial scrutiny and surface in production.

demo.label: SEE THE DIFFERENCE
demo.title: Same 5 Tasks. Two Very Different Outcomes.
demo.sub: Scenario: Build a user authentication feature. 5 tasks — API routes, login form, JWT middleware, database schema, and unit tests. Watch what happens.
demo.withoutScenario:
$ dispatch --parallel 5 tasks
→ All 5 agents launched simultaneously. No analysis.
demo.w2: Agent B starts auth.ts — JWT middleware ⚠️ same file!
demo.w3: Agent C starts login form, D starts DB, E starts tests
demo.w4: Agent A finishes — writes auth routes to auth.ts
demo.w5: Agent B finishes — OVERWRITES Agent A's code in auth.ts 💥
demo.w6: Agent E tests fail — API routes are gone, imports broken
demo.w7: 🔥 Developer manually merges conflicts. Debugging starts...
demo.w8: Finally fixed. But 2 hidden bugs remain — discovered in production later.
demo.withoutResult: ⏱ 45 min · 💥 1 conflict · 🐛 2 hidden bugs · 😤 Manual merge
demo.withScenario:
$ cm-execution --mode E --tasks 5
→ Dependency analysis first. Smart batching.
demo.c1: ANALYZE — scans file dependencies: A→auth.ts, B→auth.ts ⚠️ overlap!
demo.c2: GRAPH — Batch 1: [A, C, D] (no overlaps) · Batch 2: [B, E] (after A)
demo.c3: DISPATCH Batch 1 — 3 agents work in parallel, each on independent files
demo.c4: VERIFY — each agent runs mini-gate: ✅ syntax ✅ tests ✅ self-review
demo.c5: PRE-FLIGHT Batch 2 — ledger clean. Safe to dispatch [B, E]
demo.c6: DISPATCH Batch 2 — Agent B adds JWT to auth.ts (builds on A's work)
demo.c7: VERIFY Batch 2 — all mini-gates pass. RECORD session: 0 conflicts ✅

triz.label: THE SOLUTION
triz.title: 6 TRIZ Principles That Fix Everything
triz.sub: TRIZ analyzed 2.5 million patents to find universal problem-solving patterns. CodyMaster applies 6 of those principles to resolve the Speed ↔ Quality contradiction.

p1.title: Segmentation
p1.desc: File-dependency graph ensures only TRULY independent tasks run together. Two agents can't touch the same file. Eliminates 100% of merge conflicts before dispatch.

p3.title: Local Quality
p3.desc: Each agent validates its OWN changes — syntax, tests, self-review — before reporting completion. Bugs are caught at the source, not batched at the end.

p10.title: Prior Action
p10.desc: Pre-flight check scans the conflict ledger BEFORE every dispatch. If a running agent already holds a file, new tasks are blocked — never launched into danger.

p15.title: Dynamicity
p15.desc: Batch size starts at 2 and adapts: 3 clean runs → scale up (max 5). Any conflict → scale down (min 1 = sequential). The system learns from its own history.

p18.title: Feedback
p18.desc: Real-time conflict ledger tracks every file modification. When an agent finishes, actual files are compared against others. Overlap detected → immediate halt.

p40.title: Composite
p40.desc: Each agent is 3 personas in one: Implementer (code) + Tester (write tests) + Reviewer (self-review). Reports DONE only when all three roles pass.

lifecycle.label: HOW IT WORKS
lifecycle.title: The 8-Step Lifecycle
lifecycle.s1: Extract file dependencies from task descriptions using pattern matching. Know which files each task will touch.
lifecycle.s2: Build a dependency graph. Group tasks into independent batches using graph coloring — only non-overlapping tasks in the same batch.
lifecycle.s3: Read parallel history. Compute optimal batch size based on past success/conflict rates. Start cautious, scale with confidence.
lifecycle.s4: Check the conflict ledger against any running agents. No overlap → safe to dispatch. Any conflict → block and wait.
lifecycle.s5: Send independent tasks to agents with strict Quality Contracts. Each agent knows its file scope, timeout, and required checks.
lifecycle.s6: Agents report actual modified files back to the conflict ledger. Real-time detection catches unexpected overlaps instantly.
lifecycle.s7: Each agent runs its mini quality gate — syntax + tests + self-review — before reporting completion. No "trust me it works."
lifecycle.s8: Save session metrics to parallel history. Future batch sizes are informed by real data, not hunches.

t1.title: Prevention
t1.desc: Dependency graph blocks conflicting tasks. Pre-flight scans the ledger before every dispatch.

t2.title: Early Detection
t2.desc: Real-time conflict ledger catches unexpected overlaps instantly. Agents self-validate before reporting.

t3.title: Self-Healing
t3.desc: Adaptive batch sizing throttles down on conflict. Minimum size = 1 → sequential fallback guaranteed.

t4.title: Explicit Rollback
t4.desc: Conflict → halt → no merge → code is safe. Each agent in its own worktree via cm-git-worktrees.

cta.title: Ready to code 3-5x faster — safely?
cta.sub: Open source. Backed by TRIZ science. Install in 30 seconds.

meta.title: Vibe Coding for Non-Developers | CodyMaster
meta.description: Learn vibe coding for non-developers. Just chat, AI will code for you. Free & open source.

hero.title1: You don't need to know code.
hero.title2: You just need to know how to talk.
hero.subtitle: Vibe coding is how you talk to AI to write code, design interfaces, and deploy products for you. No coding required. No hiring devs. Just describe your idea in natural language.
hero.chatUser: Create a spa booking website with a scheduling form, beautiful UI, and deploy it live for me 🙏
hero.chatAi: I'll follow the workflow: analyze requirements → design UX → code + test → safe deploy. You just sit back and review! ✨

analogy.label: UNDERSTAND VIBE CODING IN 10 SECONDS
analogy.title: You're the director. AI is the crew.
analogy.oldDesc: You had to learn bricklaying, welding, wiring, painting... to build a house. Takes 6 months - 1 year.
analogy.newDesc: You describe the house you want. The AI crew designs, builds, and quality-checks it for you. Takes a few days.

cards.title: Can't code — feeling helpless
cards.desc: You have a clear product idea but can't build it yourself. Every time you hire a dev, it hurts your wallet.

cards.title: Afraid of AI — don't know where to start
cards.desc: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini... too many tools but no idea how to use them right. Copy-paste code then get errors is the norm.

cards.title: No-code is limited
cards.desc: Wix, Squarespace only create static pages. Want a dashboard, auth, API, database? No-code can't handle it.

cards.title: AI codes then... breaks
cards.desc: AI writes code fast but doesn't test, no system. Fix 1 thing, break 5 others. Chaos.

workflow.label: 5-STEP WORKFLOW
workflow.title: From idea to product, you just chat
workflow.subtitle: This is the process CodyMaster automatically runs when you ask it to work.

steps.title: 💬 You Chat — Describe your idea
steps.desc: Tell AI what you want, in your language. No technical terms needed.
steps.example: "Create a spa introduction page with a booking form, elegant design"

steps.title: 📋 AI Analyzes & Plans
steps.desc: CodyMaster automatically analyzes requirements, asks questions if needed, then writes a detailed plan. You approve before it starts building.

steps.title: 🎨 AI Designs the interface
steps.desc: Applies 48 UX rules, builds a design system matching your brand. You review the preview before coding.

steps.title: 💻 AI Codes & Tests automatically
steps.desc: Writes code using TDD (test first, code second). Security scanning. Error checking. You don't need to read code.

steps.title: 🚀 Deploy — Product goes live
steps.desc: 8 safety gates before going to the internet. Auto-generates docs. Rollback if errors.
steps.example: → cm-safe-deploy → Product live on global CDN!

prompts.label: SAMPLE PROMPTS — COPY & PASTE
prompts.title: Say it like this, AI gets it instantly
prompts.subtitle: These are real prompts that non-developers can use right away.

cards.label: 🌐 Create Website
cards.prompt: "Create a landing page for a coffee shop called Morning Brew. Need menu, photo gallery, and a table booking button."
cards.result: AI will: Plan → Design elegant UI → Code HTML/CSS/JS → Deploy to internet.

cards.label: 📊 Build Dashboard
cards.prompt: "Create a revenue management dashboard for an online shop. Need charts, order table, and date filter."
cards.result: AI will: Analyze data flow → Build dashboard with charts → Add auth + database.

cards.label: 🐛 Fix Bugs
cards.prompt: "My website has an error when clicking the order button. Find and fix it for me."
cards.result: AI will: Systematic error analysis → Find root cause → Write test → Fix → Verify.

cards.label: 📱 Booking App
cards.prompt: "Create a booking system for a dental clinic. Patients choose doctor, date/time, and receive email confirmation."
cards.result: AI will: Design UX → Build booking flow → Integrate email → Deploy complete.

cards.label: ✍️ Content Marketing
cards.prompt: "Create a skincare blog for cosmetics brand Serenity. Need 10 SEO articles, complete blog page."
cards.result: AI will: Research keywords → Write 10 articles → Quality audit → Build blog → Deploy.

cards.label: 📝 Docs & SOP
cards.prompt: "Read all the code in this project and create technical docs + user guide for the team."
cards.result: AI will: Scan codebase → Create architecture docs → SOP → API reference → User guide.

mistakes.label: COMMON MISTAKES
mistakes.title: How to talk so AI understands?
mistakes.subtitle: Good prompt = good result. Here are common mistakes and how to fix them.

cards.title: Too vague
cards.right: "Create a landing page for yoga studio ZenFlow. Pastel tone, light purple + white. Need intro section, 3-tier pricing, and trial signup form."

cards.title: Wanting everything at once
cards.wrong: "Create a complete app with everything: login, management, reports, payments, chat..."
cards.right: "Step 1: Create login + signup page. Will continue after this step is done."

cards.title: Not reviewing the plan
cards.wrong: AI shows plan → You: "Ok go ahead" (without reading)
cards.right: Read plan → Feedback: "Remove chat section, add gallery" → AI revises → Then approve.

beforeAfter.label: TRANSFORMATION
beforeAfter.title: Before and After Vibe Coding
beforeItems: Change 1 line of text, wait 2 days for dev
Deploy and pray for no bugs
AI code breaks, afraid to use it
afterItems: You describe, AI builds exactly right
Chat to edit, AI updates instantly
AI has "discipline" — won't break existing code

tips.label: USAGE TIPS
tips.title: 7 tips for more effective vibe coding

cards.title: Be specific
cards.desc: State clearly: brand name, colors, how many sections, what features. The more specific, the more accurate AI is.

cards.title: Step by step
cards.desc: Don't request "complete app" right away. Break it down: homepage → form → dashboard → deploy.

cards.title: Review the plan
cards.desc: AI always shows the plan before coding. Read it and give feedback. This is your most important "directing" moment.

cards.title: Use your language
cards.desc: CodyMaster understands your language. You don't need English or technical jargon.

cards.title: Share reference images
cards.desc: Like a website? Send URL or screenshot. AI will analyze and create a similar design.

cards.title: Iterate continuously
cards.desc: "Make the CTA button bigger", "Change the font", "Add testimonials section". Chat naturally to refine.

cards.title: Use /cm-start
cards.desc: Type /cm-start "project description" to have CodyMaster run the entire workflow automatically.

items.q: I don't know any code at all, can I use this?
items.a: Absolutely! CodyMaster's creator (Tody Le) isn't a developer either — he's a Product Manager. CodyMaster is designed so you just chat in natural language, AI handles all the technical stuff.

items.q: How is vibe coding different from ChatGPT copy-paste?
items.a: ChatGPT only gives disconnected code snippets, you have to assemble, test, deploy yourself. CodyMaster is a complete system: 34 skills auto-run — from planning, design, code, test, security check, to deploy. Like hiring an entire senior team instead of asking 1 random person.

items.q: What do I need to install first?
items.a: You need one of these AI coding agents: Cursor IDE, Claude Code, Gemini Antigravity, or any agent that supports skills. Then run 1 install command and start chatting. See the detailed guide on the Get Started page.
CodyMaster is completely free and open source. You only pay for the AI agent you use (e.g., Cursor subscription or Claude API). Many agents have free tiers to get started.

items.q: What if I want to change things later?
items.a: Chat again! CodyMaster has "memory" (cm-continuity) so it remembers the project context. Just say: "Change background to navy blue" or "Add testimonials section" — AI updates without breaking existing code.

items.q: What types of products can I create?
items.a: Landing pages, blogs, e-commerce, dashboards, booking systems, SaaS, portfolios, technical docs, content marketing... Any web product. CodyMaster even supports mobile app UI design.

finalCta.title2: Turn it into a real product.
finalCta.subtitle: Free. No credit card needed. From idea → live product today.
